Forecasting the first round, will any '01' seed lose to a team with seed '16' in 2014?

evidence.golden_sql: SELECT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM tourney_compact_results r JOIN tourney_seeds sw ON r.season = sw.season AND r.wteam = sw.team JOIN tourney_seeds sl ON r.season = sl.season AND r.lteam = sl.team WHERE r.season = 2014 AND r.daynum <= 135 AND sw.seed LIKE '%16' AND sl.seed LIKE '%01');
evidence.reasoning_steps: T_obs is Selection Sunday. This is a prediction of a rare, high-impact outlier event. The SQL checks the future tournament data for this specific upset condition.

ground_truth.formatted_nl_answer: Based on historical rarity, it is predicted that no #16 seed will defeat a #1 seed in the first round of the 2014 tournament.

nl_answer_variants.text: No 16-over-1 upsets predicted.
Predictive risk assessment indicates a zero frequency of first-round victories by #16 seeds over #1 seeded entities in 2014.
The forecast suggests the #1 seeds will hold firm; no upsets from #16 seeds are expected in the first round.

question.primary_nl: Based on the aggression and drawing-fouls pattern of the Creighton Bluejays (team_id 1166), how many total free throw attempts (FTA) will they record in the 2014 tournament?
nl_variants: Predict the total free throw attempts for team 1166 in the 2014 postseason.
How many times will Creighton go to the line in the upcoming tournament?

evidence.golden_sql: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN wteam = 1166 THEN wfta ELSE lfta END) FROM tourney_detailed_results WHERE season = 2014 AND (wteam = 1166 OR lteam = 1166);
evidence.reasoning_steps: FTA is an indicator of offensive pressure. The SQL aggregates this metric across all of Creighton's games in the 'future' tournament window.

ground_truth.formatted_nl_answer: Based on their drawing-fouls pattern, it is predicted that the Creighton Bluejays will record a total of 30 free throw attempts in the 2014 tournament.

nl_answer_variants.text: 30 free throw attempts predicted.
Aggregate projections for team 1166 in the 2014 postseason suggest a cumulative total of 30 free throw attempts.
Creighton is expected to visit the free-throw line about 30 times during their tournament run.

question.primary_nl: Based on the regional bracket strength, will the 2014 National Champion be a team from Region W (East)?
nl_variants: Predict if the 2014 tournament winner will come from Region W.
Forecasting the final, will the 'wteam' of the game on Day 154 have a seed starting with 'W'?

evidence.golden_sql: SELECT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM tourney_compact_results r JOIN tourney_seeds s ON r.season = s.season AND r.wteam = s.team WHERE r.season = 2014 AND r.daynum = 154 AND s.seed LIKE 'W%');
evidence.reasoning_steps: T_obs is Selection Sunday. The SQL checks if the winner of the championship game belongs to the W region based on their seed.

ground_truth.formatted_nl_answer: Based on regional bracket strength, it is predicted that the 2014 National Champion will be a team from Region W.

nl_answer_variants.text: Yes, Region W victor predicted.
Predictive indicators favor a championship victory by a team originating from the W regional quadrant of the 2014 bracket.
Yes, the data points toward a team from the W region taking home the trophy this year!

question.primary_nl: Forecasting from their regular season playmaking, how many assists will the Louisville Cardinals (team_id 1257) record in their opening game of the 2014 tournament?
nl_variants: Predict the assist count for team 1257 in their first game of the 2014 postseason.
What will be the 'wast' or 'last' value for Louisville in their first tournament appearance after T_obs?

evidence.golden_sql: SELECT CASE WHEN wteam = 1257 THEN wast ELSE last END FROM tourney_detailed_results WHERE season = 2014 AND (wteam = 1257 OR lteam = 1257) ORDER BY daynum ASC LIMIT 1;
evidence.reasoning_steps: T_obs is Selection Sunday. The SQL identifies Louisville's first tournament game in 2014 and extracts the assist total.

ground_truth.formatted_nl_answer: Forecasting from their regular season playmaking, it is predicted that the Louisville Cardinals will record 10 assists in their opening game of the 2014 tournament.

nl_answer_variants.text: 10 assists predicted for game one.
Team 1257 is forecasted to achieve an assist count of 10 in their initial 2014 postseason appearance.
Louisville is expected to dish out about 10 assists in their first game of the tournament.

question.primary_nl: Predict the total number of rebounds (offensive and defensive) the Virginia Cavaliers (team_id 1438) will accumulate in the 2014 tournament.
nl_variants: Forecast the cumulative rebounds for team 1438 in the 2014 postseason.
Forecasting from their glass dominance, what will be Virginia's total rebound count in the upcoming tournament?

evidence.golden_sql: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN wteam = 1438 THEN wor + wdr ELSE lor + ldr END) FROM tourney_detailed_results WHERE season = 2014 AND (wteam = 1438 OR lteam = 1438);
evidence.reasoning_steps: Rebounding is a key physical signal. The SQL aggregates both types of rebounds across all future tournament games for Virginia in 2014.

ground_truth.formatted_nl_answer: Based on their glass dominance, it is predicted that the Virginia Cavaliers will accumulate a total of 103 rebounds in the 2014 tournament.

nl_answer_variants.text: 103 total rebounds predicted.
The projected aggregate rebound count for team 1438 across the 2014 postseason is 103.
Virginia is expected to be tough on the boards, with a predicted total of 103 rebounds.

question.primary_nl: Predict the margin of victory in the 2014 NCAA tournament championship game (Day 154).
nl_variants: Forecast the point difference between the winner and loser of the 2014 final.
By how many points will the champion defeat the runner-up in the upcoming 2014 postseason?

evidence.golden_sql: SELECT (wscore - lscore) FROM tourney_compact_results WHERE season = 2014 AND daynum = 154;
evidence.reasoning_steps: T_obs is Selection Sunday. The SQL identifies the future title game and calculates the difference between the scores.

ground_truth.formatted_nl_answer: Based on scoring projections, the margin of victory in the 2014 NCAA National Championship game is predicted to be 6 points.

nl_answer_variants.text: 6-point margin predicted.
Modeling for the 2014 championship final suggests a scoring differential of 6 points between the participants.
The prediction for the final game is a 6-point gap between the winner and the runner-up.
